heading: Mining laws relating to placer claims extended to Alaska
label: 49b Mining laws relating to placer claims extended to Alaska
text: The general mining laws of the United States so far as they are applicable to placer-mining claims, as prior to , extended to the Territory of Alaska, are declared to be in full force and effect in said Territory: , That nothing herein shall be held to change or affect the rights acquired by locators or owners of placer-mining claims prior to , located in said Territory under act (, 243) and amendatory act ().May 4, 1934May 4, 1934August 1, 1912March 3, 1925Provided37 Stat. 24243 Stat. 1118
notes: References in TextAct (, 243) and amenda­tory act (), referred to in text, were repealed by section 1 of act . See sections 35 to 37 and 49b of this title.August 1, 1912March 3, 1925May 4, 193437 Stat. 24243 Stat. 1118
CodificationSection was formerly classified to sections 119 and 381a of Title 48, Territories and Insular Possessions.

Effective Date, , provided that:Act May 4, 1934, ch. 211, § 348 Stat. 663“This Act [enacting this section] shall take effect thirty days subsequent to the date of convening of the first regular session of the Alaska Territorial Legislature which is held after the passage of this Act [].”May 4, 1934

Admission of Alaska as StateAdmission of Alaska into the Union was accomplished , on issuance of Proc.
